In fact, what is surprising to me is that the confidence level is below or almost matching what it had been 15 years ago when we had the Great Recession Massive Job Losses and foreclosure crisis. We know home buying is a major decision, so without that confidence, it could delay or postpone some of that home purchase decisions. Now, the mortgage rate I have found that has a bigger influence can counter the confidence factor. So if somehow the mortgage rate was to retreat down towards three-year low, four-year low condition, maybe even with a weaker confidence, we still have some decent home buying activity.
Cape Coral_FL.script: Consumer confidence right now is at levels we haven't seen since the Great Recession. And that's wild because we don't have mass layoffs. We don't have foreclosures piling up. The job market is soft but people still have jobs. So why does this matter to you as a Cape Coral buyer? Because low confidence makes people hesitate, and hesitation in this market creates opportunity. Here's the thing though: mortgage rates have historically been a stronger trigger than confidence. When rates dropped to three and four year lows in past cycles, buyers came back even when the headlines were scary. Cape Coral inventory is sitting right now, sellers are negotiating, and if rates tick down even slightly this summer, that window closes fast. The buyers who moved during low confidence periods are the ones who got the best deals. Are you planning to buy in Cape Coral this year or waiting? Right before your Instagram breakthrough as a realtor, you’re going to have these three weird urges.

1️⃣ You’ll want to completely change everything.
If you’ve been building on Instagram, suddenly you’ll think this isn’t working and you need to start cold calling FSBOs because you saw one reel of an agent having wild success and now that feels like the only way to win.

2️⃣ You’ll want to quit.
Deals will fall apart and it will feel like everything you’ve been doing is in vain.
You’ll start questioning if this is even what God called you to do and convince yourself maybe you were only meant to stay home and this dream was a distraction.

3️⃣ You’ll start complaining.
You’ll catch yourself saying things like you hate real estate, other agents are the worst, buyers are liars.
And without realizing it, you’re speaking things that are tearing down your mindset.

All of this is normal.
Every single one of us has been there.

The difference between the ones who make it and the ones who don’t is not that they don’t have these thoughts.

They just don’t let those thoughts lead.

They push through or they choose to trust God in the process.

Pick your hard.

God never promised this would be easy.
He said in this world you will have trouble but take heart I have overcome the world.

This work matters but it’s also just a blip compared to eternity.

That perspective has helped me let things roll off my back and stay focused on what God has actually called me to do. 🤍
